mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 7.0% signals a tightening labor market, boosting corporate earnings and consumer spending, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 7.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) signals robust supply-side expansion, reducing recession risks and boosting equity valuations, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between INDPRO and GDPC1 fails to produce a positive 60d return in 50%+ of cases where both series show growth.

mechanism_narrative: In monetary tightening cycles, rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) signal reduced liquidity, increasing equity discount rates and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts (HOUST > 650k) reflects strong demand for residential construction, boosting related sectors and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 50%+ of instances where HOUST exceeds 650k.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) triggers central bank hawkishness, increasing bond yields and reducing equity valuations, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in consumer spending (RSAFS > 400k) and employment (PAYEMS > 130k) signals strong demand-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between RSAFS and PAYEMS fails to produce a positive 60d return in 50%+ of cases where both series show growth.

mechanism_narrative: In supply shock regimes, declining industrial production (INDPRO < 95) signals reduced capacity utilization and higher input costs, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO falls below 95.
